description: Pain or pressure in the head; often from stress, dehydration, or eyestrain.
worstCase: Severe migraine or cluster headache may require medical attention if persistent or accompanied by vision changes.
remedy:
• Rest in a quiet, dark room
• Hydrate
• Cold compress or gentle temple massage
• OTC pain relief if safe
(Do: Take breaks; Don't: Skip meals or overexert)

description: Elevated body temperature, usually due to infection.
worstCase: High fever (>104°F/40°C) or fever with rash, confusion, or breathing difficulty requires urgent medical care.
remedy:
• Drink water or electrolyte fluids
• Rest and sleep
• Lukewarm sponge bath
• Paracetamol if safe
(Do: Monitor temperature; Don't: Overdress)

description: Mild viral infection of nose/throat; symptoms include cough, congestion, sneezing.
worstCase: If symptoms worsen with high fever, chest pain, or shortness of breath, see a doctor.
remedy:
• Rest and hydrate
• Saline nasal drops or steam
• Honey or warm teas for throat
(Do: Wash hands; Don't: Use antibiotics unnecessarily)

description: Upper abdominal discomfort or burning, often after meals.
worstCase: Persistent pain, vomiting blood, or black stools may indicate ulcers or GI issues—seek medical care.
remedy:
• Eat small, bland meals
• Avoid spicy/fried foods
• Drink warm water or fennel tea
(Do: Eat upright; Don't: Overeat)

description: Persistent tiredness not relieved by rest.
worstCase: Extreme fatigue with dizziness, shortness of breath, or palpitations may indicate anemia, thyroid, or heart issues.
remedy:
• 7–9 hours sleep
• Balanced diet, iron-rich foods
• Light exercise
(Do: Rest when needed; Don't: Overexert)

description: Frequent watery stools caused by infection or diet.
worstCase: Severe dehydration or diarrhea lasting >48 hrs requires medical attention.
remedy:
• Hydrate (water/ORS)
• Eat bland foods (BRAT diet)
• Avoid dairy or spicy foods
(Do: Rehydrate; Don't: Eat junk food)

description: Immune reaction to allergens like pollen, dust, or pets.
worstCase: Severe allergy can trigger breathing difficulties (anaphylaxis) — call emergency services.
remedy:
• Avoid triggers
• Saline rinse or antihistamines
• Keep windows closed
(Do: Clean rooms; Don't: Rub eyes)

description: Queasy feeling in stomach; may precede vomiting.
worstCase: Persistent vomiting causing dehydration or blood in vomit needs urgent care.
remedy:
• Sip clear fluids
• Eat mild foods
• Avoid greasy foods
(Do: Rest upright; Don't: Force eat)

description: Inflammation of hair follicles; common in teens and adults.
worstCase: Severe cystic acne may require dermatologist treatment to prevent scarring.
remedy:
• Gentle cleansing, avoid harsh scrubbing
• Topical treatments (benzoyl peroxide/salicylic acid)
(Do: Keep skin clean; Don't: Pick pimples)

description: Pain in lower or upper back, often from posture or strain.
worstCase: Severe pain with numbness, weakness, or bladder issues may indicate spinal nerve problem.
remedy:
• Gentle stretching, posture correction
• Heat/ice therapy
• OTC pain relief
(Do: Stay active; Don't: Lift heavy suddenly)

description: Acid from stomach rising into esophagus causing burning.
worstCase: Persistent reflux or difficulty swallowing may indicate esophageal damage — see doctor.
remedy:
• Avoid late-night meals
• Eat small meals, avoid spicy/fatty food
• Elevate head while sleeping
(Do: Track triggers; Don't: Lie immediately after eating)

description: Feelings of worry or nervousness affecting daily life.
worstCase: Severe anxiety attacks affecting breathing or daily function may require professional help.
remedy:
• Deep breathing, meditation
• Light exercise, balanced sleep
• Talk therapy if needed
(Do: Relaxation; Don't: Overthink excessively)

description: Excessive hair shedding, often due to genetics, stress, or illness.
worstCase: Rapid or patchy hair loss may need dermatologist evaluation.
remedy:
• Maintain nutrition and protein intake
• Avoid harsh chemicals
(Do: Gentle care; Don't: Pull or scratch scalp)

worstCase: Chronic insomnia may affect mood, memory, and health; seek professional help if persistent.
remedy:
• Maintain sleep schedule
• Avoid caffeine or screens before bed
• Relaxation techniques
(Do: Sleep hygiene; Don't: Stress about sleep)
